story.title: Dr. Washington and the Case of the Slow Domino
story.text:
My name is Dr. Anika Washington. I am a collision physics researcher. I study how energy moves when things crash. We can see this in a chain reaction, like falling dominoes.

I give the first domino a push. This gives it motion energy. It falls and hits the next domino. This is a collision. The first domino passes its energy to the second. This is kinetic energy transfer. The energy travels down the line from one domino to the next.

But does all the energy make it to the end? No. Listen when the dominoes fall. You hear a 'clack' sound at each impact. Sound is a form of energy. Each 'clack' is energy loss. Some motion energy turns into sound energy.

This is why the last domino falls slowly. A little energy is lost at every step. If the dominoes have more mass, they are harder to move. They use up more energy. My work helps people understand how energy moves. We can use this to build safer cars.
think_abouts: What sounds do you hear when dominoes fall?
Why do you think heavier objects are harder to push?
story.go_deeper: What might happen if the dominoes were made of soft rubber instead of hard plastic?

margin_defs.definition: when two things hit each other
the power to do work or cause change

check_prompts: Where does some of the motion energy go when two dominoes hit each other?

vocabulary.definition: When two objects hit each other, transferring energy from one to the other.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The collision between the two balls made a clicking sound.

vocabulary.definition: When a moving object hits another object and passes its motion energy to it.
vocabulary.use_sentence: We saw kinetic energy transfer when the first domino hit the second one.

vocabulary.definition: Energy that changes from motion into sound or heat during a collision.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The sound of the dominoes hitting each other is a type of energy loss.

vocabulary.definition: How much 'stuff' is in an object. Heavier objects have more mass.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A bowling ball has more mass than a beach ball.

vocabulary.definition: A series of events where each event triggers the next one, like falling dominoes.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Pushing the first domino started a long chain reaction.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Washington studies dominoes to understand bigger things like car crashes. What other chain reactions can you think of in your daily life?
discussion_cards.frame: One example of a chain reaction I see is ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: If you wanted the last domino to fall faster, what is one thing you could change about your setup? Why?
discussion_cards.frame: To make the last domino fall faster, I would change ___ because ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: The story says energy is lost as sound. What are other ways energy might be 'lost' from the moving dominoes?
discussion_cards.frame: Besides sound, energy could also be lost as ___.

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
